skill.name: writing-clearly-and-concisely
skill.description: Apply Strunk's timeless writing rules to ANY prose humans will read—documentation, commit messages, error messages, explanations, reports, or UI text. Makes your writing clearer, stronger, and more professional.
skill.summary: Improve human-facing prose with concise, practical style rules.
skill.author: Cygnusfear

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported filesystem, external command, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance patterns. Manual review found these are false positives from Markdown prose, inline filenames, grammar examples, and literary text. No executable code, network access, secret access, prompt injection, or malicious intent was found.

low_findings.title: False positive: inline Markdown filenames
low_findings.description: The reported Ruby or shell backtick execution locations are inline Markdown references to elements-of-style.md and prose about using a subagent. They do not execute commands or interpolate user input.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The marked backticks delimit Markdown inline code for filenames and ordinary instructions. No shell, Ruby, or command execution syntax is present in the reviewed context.

low_findings.title: False positive: hard link creation wording
low_findings.description: The filesystem findings occur in grammar examples using the word Lincoln. They are not calls to link, ln, or any filesystem API.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The relevant lines are prose examples from a style manual. They contain no code, command syntax, or filesystem operation.

low_findings.title: False positive: weak cryptography keywords in prose
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography findings are ordinary words in writing guidance, such as hence, thus, while, and similar prose. No hashing, encryption, or cryptographic API use was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Reviewed contexts are natural-language writing rules and descriptions. There is no implementation code or cryptographic behavior to evaluate.

low_findings.title: False positive: reconnaissance keywords in examples
low_findings.description: The reconnaissance findings are words embedded in grammar rules, table examples, and public-domain prose. They do not collect system details, run discovery commands, or access local environment data.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The locations are natural-language examples and section summaries. No commands, APIs, or instructions gather host, network, user, or environment information.

content.user_title: Edit Prose With Clear Style Rules
content.value_statement: Unclear writing slows reviews, support, and implementation work. This skill applies practical style rules to make prose shorter, clearer, and easier to act on.
actual_capabilities: Applies concise writing principles to documentation, reports, messages, and explanations.
Uses active voice, concrete wording, and direct sentence structure.
Identifies needless words and suggests shorter revisions.
Improves commit messages, pull request text, error messages, and UI copy.
Provides a fallback rule set when the full guide is too large for context.
limitations: It is a writing guide, not an automated grammar checker.
It relies on older style guidance that may need adaptation for inclusive language.
It does not verify technical accuracy in the source text.
It may shorten prose too much if tone or detail requirements are not stated.

use_cases.title: Improve Technical Documentation
use_cases.description: Revise README sections, API explanations, and guides so readers can scan and understand them faster.

use_cases.title: Tighten Product Copy
use_cases.description: Edit UI text, help messages, and release notes to remove vague language and needless words.

use_cases.title: Polish Team Communication
use_cases.description: Make commit messages, pull request summaries, and status updates direct, specific, and professional.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Copyedit
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the writing-clearly-and-concisely skill to edit this paragraph for clarity and brevity. Keep the meaning unchanged: [paste text]

prompt_templates.title: Documentation Rewrite
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the writing-clearly-and-concisely skill to rewrite this documentation. Prefer active voice, concrete nouns, and shorter sentences. Preserve all technical details: [paste text]

prompt_templates.title: Before And After Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the writing-clearly-and-concisely skill to improve this text. Return the revised version and a short list of the most important edits: [paste text]

prompt_templates.title: Audience-Specific Edit
prompt_templates.scenario: Tune a larger draft for a defined audience.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the writing-clearly-and-concisely skill to edit this draft for [audience]. Keep the tone [tone], preserve required terms, and flag any sentence where clarity conflicts with precision: [paste text]

output_examples.input: The implementation of the feature was completed by the team and there are several improvements that were made.
output_examples.output: The team completed the feature and made several improvements.

output_examples.input: Due to the fact that the request was not valid, the operation could not be completed successfully.
output_examples.output: The operation failed because the request was invalid.

output_examples.input: It should be noted that users have the ability to configure settings in order to change notification behavior.
output_examples.output: Users can configure settings to change notification behavior.

best_practices: State the target audience and desired tone before asking for an edit.
Ask the model to preserve technical terms, names, and compliance language.
Review shortened text to confirm that important context remains.
anti_patterns: Do not use it to replace factual review by a domain expert.
Do not ask for a shorter version without naming any required details.
Do not apply older usage rules blindly when inclusive or modern wording matters.

faq.question: What does this skill help me write?
faq.answer: It helps improve documentation, commit messages, UI copy, reports, explanations, and other prose for human readers.

faq.question: Does it change the meaning of my text?
faq.answer: It aims to preserve meaning while improving clarity. You should review technical or legal text before publishing.

faq.question: Can it handle long documents?
faq.answer: Yes, but the guide is large. For long drafts, edit sections or use the limited context strategy in the skill.

faq.question: Does it check grammar?
faq.answer: It includes grammar and punctuation guidance, but its main focus is clear, concise prose.

faq.question: Can Claude, Codex, and Claude Code use it?
faq.answer: Yes. The metadata lists Claude, Codex, and Claude Code as supported tools.

faq.question: Is this skill safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. Manual review found only Markdown prose and public-domain writing guidance, with no executable behavior.
